devanagari: भगवता सौभसहितस्य शाल्वस्य विनाशः । श्रीशुक उवाच । स तूपस्पृश्य सलिलं दंशितो धृतकार्मुकः । नय मां द्युमतः पार्श्वं वीरस्येत्याह सारथिम् ...
english_devnagari: bhagavatā saubhasahitasya śālvasya vināśaḥ śrīśuka uvāca sa tūpaspṛśya salilaṃ daṃśito dhṛtakārmukaḥ naya māṃ dyumataḥ pārśvaṃ vīrasyetyāha sārathim

translation: Celestial drums were sounded and showers of flowers were let down. Glorified by the sages and by bards like Sūta, Māgadha and Bandins, Hari came to his capital Dvārakā, surrounded by his armies.

devanagari: विधमन्तं स्वसैन्यानि द्युमन्तं रुक्मिणीसुतः । प्रतिहत्य प्रत्यविध्यन् नाराचैरष्टभिः स्मयन् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: vidhamantaṃ svasainyāni dyumantaṃ rukmiṇīsutaḥ pratihatya pratyavidhyan nārācairaṣṭabhiḥ smayan

translation: He entered the charming city decorated with a number of flags and other decorative work, and with clean quadrangles besprinkled with water.

devanagari: चतुर्भिश्चतुरो वाहान् सूतमेकेन चाहनत् । द्वावाभ्यं धनुश्च केतुं च शरेणान्येन वै शिरः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: caturbhiścaturo vāhān sūtamekena cāhanat dvāvābhyaṃ dhanuśca ketuṃ ca śareṇānyena vai śiraḥ

translation: He (Kṛṣṇa) was honoured by elderly Yādavas as well as by men in the city and the rural areas. He delighted the sixteen thousand queens who were over-whelmed with love for him. Thus the son of Devakī lived happily and in joy, O descendant of Kurus.

devanagari: गदसात्यकिसाम्बाद्या जघ्नुः सौभपतेर्बलम् । पेतुः समुद्रे सौभेयाः सर्वे संछिन्नकन्धराः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: gadasātyakisāmbādyā jaghnuḥ saubhapaterbalam petuḥ samudre saubheyāḥ sarve saṃchinnakandharāḥ
translation: All the celestials in their aerial cars felt scorched with the heat of the discus Sudarśana, cleared out of the sky and fled in all directions.

devanagari: एवं यदूनां शाल्वानां निघ्नतामितरेतरम् । युद्धं त्रिणवरात्रं तद् अभूत् तुमुलमुल्बणम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ yadūnāṃ śālvānāṃ nighnatāmitaretaram yuddhaṃ triṇavarātraṃ tad abhūt tumulamulbaṇam

translation: The Sudarśana discus dazzling like a hundred suns cut asunder the city of Saubha in two and again returned to the hand of Nārāyaṇa, O king Parīkṣit.

devanagari: इन्द्रप्रस्थं गतः कृष्ण आहूतो धर्मसूनुना । राजसूयेऽथ निवृत्ते शिशुपाले च संस्थिते ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: indraprasthaṃ gataḥ kṛṣṇa āhūto dharmasūnunā rājasūye
translation: Thus the city (of Saubha) lay shattered in the sea. But the irresistible Śālva made his appearance with a mace in hand. The wicked fellow hurled his mace at Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: कुरुवृद्धाननुज्ञाप्य मुनींश्च ससुतां पृथाम् । निमित्तान्यतिघोराणि पश्यन् द्वावारवतीं ययौ ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: kuruvṛddhānanujñāpya munīṃśca sasutāṃ pṛthām nimittānyatighorāṇi paśyan dvāvāravatīṃ yayau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Getting into the chariot brought up by the Daitya army—a chariot equipped with a heap of arms—Śālva discharged innumerable weapons on?, e head of Kṛṣṇa and roared like a lion.

devanagari: आह चाहमिहायात आर्यमिश्राभिसङ्गतः । राजन्याश्चैद्यपक्षीया नूनं हन्युः पुरीं मम ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: āha cāhamihāyāta āryamiśrābhisaṅgataḥ rājanyāścaidyapakṣīyā nūnaṃ hanyuḥ purīṃ mama
translation: Hari split the mace hurled by Śālva, in three pieces with his arrows. He blew his conch Pāñcajanya and roared like a lion.

devanagari: वीक्ष्य तत्कदनं स्वानां निरूप्य पुररक्षणम् । सौभं च शाल्वराजं च दारुकं प्राह केशवः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: vīkṣya tatkadanaṃ svānāṃ nirūpya purarakṣaṇam saubhaṃ ca śālvarājaṃ ca dārukaṃ prāha keśavaḥ
translation: After his arrival at Dvārakā he noticed the havoc wrought upon his subjects. He then entrusted the defence of the city to Balarāma. Perceiving the aerial car Saubha and the King of Śālvas, he commanded Dāruka.

devanagari: रथं प्रापय मे सूत शाल्वस्यान्तिकमाशु वै । सम्भ्रमस्ते न कर्तव्यो मायावी सौभराडयम् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: rathaṃ prāpaya me sūta śālvasyāntikamāśu vai sambhramaste na kartavyo māyāvī saubharāḍayam

translation: “Quickly bring my chariot near Śālva, O charioteer. You need not be afraid (even though) this master of Saubha is skilled in the use of black magic in war-fare.”

devanagari: इत्युक्तश्चोदयामास रथमास्थाय दारुकः । विशन्तं ददृशुः सर्वे स्वे परे चारुणानुजम् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: ityuktaścodayāmāsa rathamāsthāya dārukaḥ viśantaṃ dadṛśuḥ sarve sve pare cāruṇānujam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ vac class 3 verb]

translation: Dāruka, thus commanded by Kṛṣṇa, brought the chariot near Śālva. The friends and the enemy saw him enter the battle-field.

devanagari: शाल्वश्च कृष्णमालोक्य हतप्रायबलेश्वरः । प्राहरत् कृष्णसूताय शक्तिं भीमरवां मृधे ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: śālvaśca kṛṣṇamālokya hataprāyabaleśvaraḥ prāharat kṛṣṇasūtāya śaktiṃ bhīmaravāṃ mṛdhe
translation: Śālva whose forces were practically wiped out, saw Kṛṣṇa enter the battle. At Kṛṣṇa’s charioteer he darted a lance (that sped through the air) making a roaring sound.

devanagari: तामापतन्तीं नभसि महोल्कामिव रंहसा । भासयन्तीं दिशः शौरिः सायकैः शतधाच्छिनत् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: tāmāpatantīṃ nabhasi maholkāmiva raṃhasā bhāsayantīṃ diśaḥ śauriḥ sāyakaiḥ śatadhācchinat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second single], [imperfect active third single]

translation: Observing the lance rushing through the air with great speed and illumining all the quarters like a big meteor (or firebrand), Kṛṣṇa splintered it in hundred pieces with his arrows.

devanagari: तं च षोडशभिर्विद्ध्वा बाणैः सौभं च खे भ्रमत् । अविध्यच्छरसन्दोहैः खं सूर्य इव रश्मिभिः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ ca ṣoḍaśabhirviddhvā bāṇaiḥ saubhaṃ ca khe bhramat avidhyaccharasandohaiḥ khaṃ sūrya iva raśmibhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sūr class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ sūr class 10 verb]

translation: Hitting Śālva with sixteen shafts, he pierced the aerial car Saubha which was moving through the sky, with a volley of arrows just as the sun fills the space in the sky, with his rays.

devanagari: शाल्वः शौरेस्तु दोः सव्यं शार्ङ्गं शार्ङ्गधन्वनः । बिभेद न्यपतद् हस्तात् शार्ङ्गमासीत् तदद्भुतम् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: śālvaḥ śaurestu doḥ savyaṃ śārṅgaṃ śārṅgadhanvanaḥ bibheda nyapatad hastāt śārṅgamāsīt tadadbhutam

translation: Śālva wounded the left arm of Kṛṣṇa in which he wielded the Śārṅga bow. And the Śārṅga bow fell from Kṛṣṇa’s hand. And it was indeed a miracle.

devanagari: हाहाकारो महानासीद् भूतानां तत्र पश्यताम् । निनद्य सौभराडुच्चैः इदमाह जनार्दनम् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: hāhākāro mahānāsīd bhūtānāṃ tatra paśyatām ninadya saubharāḍuccaiḥ idamāha janārdanam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [aorist active third single], [injunctive active third single]

translation: There was an outburst of wild outcry of consternation and wonder from all beings who witnessed it. The king of Saubha (i.e. Śālva) roared loudly and (boastfully) said to Kṛṣṇa:

devanagari: यत्त्वया मूढ नः सख्युः भ्रातुर्भार्या हृतेक्षताम् । प्रमत्तः स सभामध्ये त्वया व्यापादितः सखा ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: yattvayā mūḍha naḥ sakhyuḥ bhrāturbhāryā hṛtekṣatām pramattaḥ sa sabhāmadhye tvayā vyāpāditaḥ sakhā

[nominative single from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [nominative dual from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ hṛ class 1 verb]

translation: “You stupid fellow! You abducted the fiancee of our brother-like friend (Śiśupāla) in our very presence and killed our friend in the assembly at the time of Yudhiṣṭhira’s Rājasūya while he was unaware.

devanagari: तं त्वाद्य निशितैर्बाणैः अपराजितमानिनम् । नयाम्यपुनरावृत्तिं यदि तिष्ठेर्ममाग्रतः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ tvādya niśitairbāṇaiḥ aparājitamāninam nayāmyapunarāvṛttiṃ yadi tiṣṭhermamāgrataḥ
translation: In your pride you (falsely) regard yourself as invincible. Only if you stand before me (in this battle), I shall, with my sharp arrows, send you to a place (Yama’s abode) whence nobody returns.”

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । वृथा त्वं कत्थसे मन्द न पश्यस्यन्तिकेऽन्तकम् । पौरुषं दर्शयन्ति स्म शूरा न बहुभाषिणः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca vṛthā tvaṃ katthase manda na paśyasyantike pauruṣaṃ darśayanti sma śūrā na bahubhāṣiṇaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

[nominative plural from √ dṛś ], [vocative plural from √ dṛś ], [accusative plural from √ dṛś ]

translation:
The glorious Lord replied:
“You are vainly bragging, O dull-witted one! You do not perceive the god of Death standing near you. Really valiant people do not indulge in idle prattling but exhibit their valour in fighting.”

devanagari: इत्युक्त्वा भगवान् शाल्वं गदया भीमवेगया । तताड जत्रौ संरब्धः स चकम्पे वमन्नसृक् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: ityuktvā bhagavān śālvaṃ gadayā bhīmavegayā tatāḍa jatrau saṃrabdhaḥ sa cakampe vamannasṛk

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[nominative single from √ vam class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ vam class 1 verb]

translation: With these words Lord Kṛṣṇa, in extreme wrath, struck down Śālva on the collar-bone with his mace of terribly vehement force. (Under that blow) Śālva was thoroughly shaken and vomitted blood.

devanagari: गदायां सन्निवृत्तायां शाल्वस्त्वन्तरधीयत । ततो मुहूर्त आगत्य पुरुषः शिरसाच्युतम् । देवक्या प्रहितोऽस्मीति नत्वा प्राह वचो रुदन् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: gadāyāṃ sannivṛttāyāṃ śālvastvantaradhīyata tato muhūrta āgatya puruṣaḥ śirasācyutam devakyā prahito

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rud class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ rud class 2 verb]

translation: While the mace returned (to Kṛṣṇa) Śālva disappeared. Then within a short while a man approached him, bowed to him with his head and while sobbing told him, “I am sent by Devakī (with a message).

devanagari: कृष्ण कृष्ण महाबाहो पिता ते पितृवत्सल । बद्ध्वापनीतः शाल्वेन सौनिकेन यथा पशुः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa mahābāho pitā te pitṛvatsala baddhvāpanītaḥ śālvena saunikena yathā paśuḥ
translation: O Kṛṣṇa, of powerful arms. O Kṛṣṇa, so affectionate to parents! Your father is captured and dragged away by Śālva even as a butcher does to an animal to be slaughtered.”

devanagari: निशम्य विप्रियं कृष्णो मानुषीं प्रकृतिं गतः । विमनस्को घृणी स्नेहाद् बभाषे प्राकृतो यथा ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: niśamya vipriyaṃ kṛṣṇo mānuṣīṃ prakṛtiṃ gataḥ vimanasko ghṛṇī snehād babhāṣe prākṛto yathā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Hearing this unpleasant news, Kṛṣṇa exhibited His human nature. Deeply troubled at heart, despondent and overcome with filial affection, he exclaimed like an ordinary mortal.

devanagari: कथं राममसम्भ्रान्तं जित्वाजेयं सुरासुरैः । शाल्वेनाल्पीयसा नीतः पिता मे बलवान् विधिः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: kathaṃ rāmamasambhrāntaṃ jitvājeyaṃ surāsuraiḥ śālvenālpīyasā nītaḥ pitā me balavān vidhiḥ
translation: “How is it possible that unperturbable Balarāma who is invincible to gods and Asuras (together) was defeated by this despicable Śālva and my father was taken away as a captive. (Ultimately) Providence is all powerful.”

devanagari: इति ब्रुवाणे गोविन्दे सौभराट् प्रत्युपस्थितः । वसुदेवमिवानीय कृष्णं चेदमुवाच सः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: iti bruvāṇe govinde saubharāṭ pratyupasthitaḥ vasudevamivānīya kṛṣṇaṃ cedamuvāca saḥ
translation: While Govinda was uttering these words, Śālva, the Lord of Saubha, made his appearance bringing with him someone resembling Vasudeva and threatened Kṛṣṇa:

devanagari: एष ते जनिता तातो यदर्थमिह जीवसि । वधिष्ये वीक्षतस्तेऽमुं ईशश्चेत् पाहि बालिश ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: eṣa te janitā tāto yadarthamiha jīvasi vadhiṣye vīkṣataste

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ jan class 10 verb], [nominative single from √ jan ]
[nominative dual from √ tas class 4 verb], [vocative dual from √ tas class 4 verb], [accusative dual from √ tas class 4 verb], [locative single from √ tas class 4 verb]
[nominative dual from √ tas class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ tas class 4 verb], [vocative dual from √ tas class 4 verb], [accusative dual from √ tas class 4 verb]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: “This is your own father who brought you forth in this world and for whom you live. Before your very eyes, I shall slay him. Save him if you can, you childish fellow!”

devanagari: एवं निर्भर्त्स्य मायावी खड्गेनानकदुन्दुभेः । उत्कृत्य शिर आदाय खस्थं सौभं समाविशत् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ nirbhartsya māyāvī khaḍgenānakadundubheḥ utkṛtya śira ādāya khasthaṃ saubhaṃ samāviśat
translation: Having threatened him thus, Śālva, the master of black magic cut off the head (of the semblance) of Vasudeva and taking it with him, he entered his aerial car Śaubha that was near him in the air.

devanagari: ततो मुहूर्तं प्रकृतावुपप्लुतः । स्वबोध आस्ते स्वजनानुषङ्गतः । महानुभावस्तदबुध्यदासुरीं । मायां स शाल्वप्रसृतां मयोदिताम् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: tato muhūrtaṃ prakṛtāvupaplutaḥ svabodha āste svajanānuṣaṅgataḥ mahānubhāvastadabudhyadāsurīṃ māyāṃ sa śālvaprasṛtāṃ mayoditām
translation: By nature, Kṛṣṇa was the embodiment of pure knowledge. But even he was overwhelmed with human weakness and remained plunged for a while (for a muhūrta) in sorrow, due to his affection for his relative (father). Then that Lord of majestic lustre realized all that scene to be nothing but an illusion of Asura type spread by Śālva as per instructions of Maya.

devanagari: न तत्र दूतं न पितुः कलेवरं । प्रबुद्ध आजौ समपश्यदच्युतः । स्वाप्नं यथा चाम्बरचारिणं रिपुं । सौभस्थमालोक्य निहन्तुमुद्यतः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: na tatra dūtaṃ na pituḥ kalevaraṃ prabuddha ājau samapaśyadacyutaḥ svāpnaṃ yathā cāmbaracāriṇaṃ ripuṃ saubhasthamālokya nihantumudyataḥ
translation: Just as a person awakened from the dream finds the dream-scene vanished, that Immutable Lord Kṛṣṇa, after ‘waking up’ (disenchantment) from the illusory effect of Śālva’s Māyā, found that there was neither the messenger nor the dead body of his father on the battlefield. Seeing his enemy ranging through the sky in his aerial car Saubha, he determined to kill him.

devanagari: एवं वदन्ति राजर्षे ऋषयः के च नान्विताः । यत् स्ववाचो विरुध्येत नूनं ते न स्मरन्त्युत ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ vadanti rājarṣe ṛṣayaḥ ke ca nānvitāḥ yat svavāco virudhyeta nūnaṃ te na smarantyuta

[nominative single from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ smṛ class 1 verb]

translation: [1] In this way, O royal sage, some sages give an inconsistent version. They do not remember that their words will prove contradictory.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Verses 23-28 give the view of inconsistent thinkers as Bhāvāratha Dīpikā points out that Balarāma never went to Yudhiṣṭhira’s Rājasūya.
Bhāgavata Candrikā From Vyāsa’s words it is clear that Balarāma was in charge of Dvāraka in Kṛṣṇa’s absence at Rājasūya. Śālva did not invade Dvārakā—in Kṛṣṇa’s absence. Padaratnāvalī emphasizes the impossibility of Hari being deluded by the magic spell.

devanagari: क्व शोकमोहौ स्नेहो वा भयं वा येऽज्ञसम्भवाः । क्व चाखण्डितविज्ञान ज्ञानैश्वर्यस्त्वखण्डितः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: kva śokamohau sneho vā bhayaṃ vā ye kva cākhaṇḍitavijñāna jñānaiśvaryastvakhaṇḍitaḥ
translation: Where are grief, delusion, attachment or fear which are possible to be found only in the ignorant? How can these be presumed to affect the Absolute Lord, the master of Perfect knowledge, wisdom and Divine potencies of omnipotence, omnipresence.

devanagari: यत्पादसेवोर्जितयाऽत्मविद्यया । हिन्वन्त्यनाद्यात्मविपर्ययग्रहम् । लभन्त आत्मीयमनन्तमैश्वरं । कुतो नु मोहः परमस्य सद्गतेः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: yatpādasevorjitayā hinvantyanādyātmaviparyayagraham labhanta ātmīyamanantamaiśvaraṃ kuto nu mohaḥ paramasya sadgateḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

[nominative dual from √ hi class 5 verb], [nominative plural from √ hi class 5 verb], [vocative dual from √ hi class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ hi class 5 verb], [accusative dual from √ hi class 5 verb], [accusative plural from √ hi class 5 verb]

translation: Hari split the mace hurled by Śālva, in three pieces with his arrows. He blew his conch Pāñcajanya and roared like a lion.

devanagari: तं शस्त्रपूगैः प्रहरन्तमोजसा । शाल्वं शरैः शौरिरमोघविक्रमः । विद्ध्वाच्छिनद् वर्म धनुः शिरोमणिं । सौभं च शत्रोर्गदया रुरोज ह ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ śastrapūgaiḥ praharantamojasā śālvaṃ śaraiḥ śauriramoghavikramaḥ viddhvācchinad varma dhanuḥ śiromaṇiṃ saubhaṃ ca śatrorgadayā ruroja ha

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second single], [imperfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: While Śālva was vigorously attacking him with a volley of weapons, Kṛṣṇa, the descendant of Śūra, of unfailing prowess, wounded Śālva deeply with his arrows and shattered to pieces Śālva’s armour, cut down his bow and the jewel on his head, and knocked down to pieces (Sālva’s aerial car) Saubha.

devanagari: तत्कृष्णहस्तेरितया विचूर्णितं । पपात तोये गदया सहस्रधा । विसृज्य तद् भूतलमास्थितो गदां । उद्यम्य शाल्वोऽच्युतमभ्यगाद् द्रुतम् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: tatkṛṣṇahasteritayā vicūrṇitaṃ papāta toye gadayā sahasradhā visṛjya tad bhūtalamāsthito gadāṃ udyamya śālvo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[imperfect active third single], [aorist active third single]

translation: Knocked down by the mace darted by Kṛṣṇa’s hand, Saubha was splintered into thousands of pieces and fell into the water in the form of powder. Abandoning it, Śālva landed on the earth and raising his mace quickly rushed at Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: आधावतः सगदं तस्य बाहुं । भल्लेन छित्त्वाथ रथाङ्गमद्भुतम् । वधाय शाल्वस्य लयार्कसन्निभं । बिभ्रद् बभौ सार्क इवोदयाचलः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: ādhāvataḥ sagadaṃ tasya bāhuṃ bhallena chittvātha rathāṅgamadbhutam vadhāya śālvasya layārkasannibhaṃ bibhrad babhau sārka ivodayācalaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sag class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ sag class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ sag class 1 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: With a special kind of arrow called bhalla, Kṛṣṇa cut down Sālva’s arm with the mace as he was rushing against him (to assault). In order to finish with Śālva, Kṛṣṇa took up his miraculous discus Sudarśana which was dazzling like the burning sun at the time of the destruction of the universe. (With the discus held up in the position of hurling), he shone resplendent like the Eastern mountain with the disc of the rising sun on its summits.

devanagari: जहार तेनैव शिरः सकुण्डलं । किरीटयुक्तं पुरुमायिनो हरिः । वज्रेण वृत्रस्य यथा पुरन्दरो । बभूव हाहेति वचस्तदा नृणाम् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: jahāra tenaiva śiraḥ sakuṇḍalaṃ kirīṭayuktaṃ purumāyino hariḥ vajreṇa vṛtrasya yathā purandaro babhūva hāheti vacastadā nṛṇām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ yuj class 7 verb], [accusative single from √ yuj class 7 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: Just as (in ancient times) Indra chopped off the head of Vṛtra, Hari, with that very Sudarśana severed the head decked with a crown and ear-rings of Śālva of extensive capacity in black-magic. And there arose an outcry of grief (alas!) from his men.

devanagari: तस्मिन्निपतिते पापे सौभे च गदया हते । नेदुर्दुन्दुभयो राजन् दिवि देवगणेरिताः । सखीनां अपचितिं कुर्वन् दन्तवक्रो रुषाभ्यगात् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: tasminnipatite pāpe saubhe ca gadayā hate nedurdundubhayo rājan divi devagaṇeritāḥ sakhīnāṃ apacitiṃ kurvan dantavakro ruṣābhyagāt

[nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[imperfect active third single], [aorist active third single]

translation:
He (Kṛṣṇa) was honoured by elderly Yādavas as well as by men in the city and the rural areas. He delighted the sixteen thousand queens who were over-whelmed with love for him. Thus the son of Devakī lived happily and in joy, O descendant of Kurus.
[back to top]

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे उत्तरार्धे । सौभवधो नाम सप्तसप्ततितमोऽध्यायः ॥ ७७ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe uttarārdhe saubhavadho nāma saptasaptatitamo
